name: Palestinian Arabic
notes: Palestinian Arabic — South Levantine, internally diverse along an urban/rural/Bedouin axis. ق qaf reflexes are the classic three-way split: URBAN (Jerusalem, Jaffa, Nazareth koine) → /ʔ/; CENTRAL RURAL (West Bank fellahin) → /k/ (with /k/→[tʃ] palatalisation, the famous 'kaf shift'); SOUTHERN/Bedouin (Negev, Gaza periphery) → /ɡ/. All four listed (ʔ first for the prestige urban form, then k, ɡ, q). ج jim → /ʒ/ urban, /dʒ/ rural/Bedouin. ك kaf → /tʃ/ before front vowels in central rural speech. Interdentals merged in urban koine, conserved in rural/Bedouin. Source: Levantine Arabic phonology (Wikipedia); Holes (2004); Cowell (1964).

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties
sources.notes: Documents the urban/rural/Bedouin qaf split in Palestinian.

sources.author: Cowell, M.W.
sources.title: A Reference Grammar of Syrian Arabic
sources.notes: Reference for the broader South Levantine continuum.

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic
